name: RABBIT-CAGE-9
title: contract/templates/bug-template.json schema diverges from authoritative bug-filing spec and file-bug.sh output
description: The bug-template.json in contract/templates/ declares fields: schema_version, id, feature, title, severity, status, description, reproduction_steps, expected, actual, filed_date. The actual bug.json schema produced by file-bug.sh and defined in bug-filing/docs/spec/spec.md uses: name (not id), filed (not filed_date), filed_by, history array, related_feature, closed, closed_by — and has no schema_version field. The schema.json in contract/schemas/bug.json.schema.json is also inconsistent (requires id and filed_date but file-bug.sh writes name and filed). Any agent that hand-crafts a bug.json from the template produces a file that is incompatible with bug-status.sh and list-bugs.sh, which read .name, .filed, and .history. This caused the previous filing session to create structurally wrong bug.json files.

history.note: Fixed: rewrote bug-template.json to match actual file-bug.sh output schema
